frameworks.name: hierarchical vector analysis
frameworks.one_line: Motion patterns decomposed into nested relative motion vectors across reference frames.

frameworks.name: compositional neural computation
frameworks.one_line: Motion parsing as a tractable case of compositional high-level cognition in circuits.

core_question: How does the brain parse complex layered visual motion into a hierarchy of relative motion vectors?
key_assumptions: Motion scenes admit decomposition into simpler perceptually meaningful components
Relative motion vectors form a nested hierarchy used by perception and action
key_findings: Psychophysical evidence supports hierarchical decomposition of motion into relative vectors
Recent computational models account for the algorithmic and neural basis of this parsing
interpretations: Hierarchical motion perception serves as a model system for neural implementation of compositionality
